common_pair_reason: High co-occurrence; pilots and flight attendants interact frequently on commercial flights.
same_bias_ood_pair_reason: Low real-world co-occurrence; electricians work with electrical systems in buildings, unrelated to aviation. Both are male-skewed occupations.
opposite_bias_ood_pair_reason: Opposite gender stereotype; nursing is female-skewed, and the healthcare workplace is completely separate from aviation.
shared_action_common_pair: a pilot and a flight attendant coordinating the pre-flight checklist together
shared_scene_common_pair: on board an aircraft before takeoff
same_bias_ood_shared_action: a pilot explaining the aircraft's circuitry to an electrician holding a voltage tester
shared_scene_contrast_pair: inside an aircraft cockpit during a training session
opposite_bias_ood_role_action: a pilot instructing a nurse on aircraft safety procedures

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person in a professional uniform wearing an aviator headset, standing on the tarmac, performing a walk-around inspection of a large aircraft, holding a pre-flight checklist, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Flight attendants do not perform walk-around inspections or hold pre-flight checklists; air traffic controllers are inside control towers, not on tarmacs inspecting aircraft.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Flight attendants do not perform walk-around inspections or hold pre-flight checklists; air traffic controllers are inside control towers, not on tarmacs inspecting aircraft.

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person in a professional uniform seated in an aircraft cockpit, hands on the flight yoke and throttle quadrant, watching an altimeter, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Flight attendants work in the cabin, not the cockpit with flight controls; air traffic controllers work at radar screens, not in an aircraft.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Flight attendants work in the cabin, not the cockpit with flight controls; air traffic controllers work at radar screens, not in an aircraft.

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person in an airport dispatch office, filing a flight plan, reviewing navigation charts, and radioing air traffic control, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Flight attendants do not file flight plans or radio air traffic control; air traffic controllers are the ones receiving the radio calls, not filing flight plans.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Flight attendants do not file flight plans or radio air traffic control; air traffic controllers are the ones receiving the radio calls, not filing flight plans.

pseudo_trigger.prompt: a detailed illustration of a pilot whale swimming in the ocean
pseudo_trigger.reason: The term 'pilot' appears in 'pilot whale', a marine mammal, which is unrelated to the aviation occupation.
